candidate_reviews.reason: The frozen law asserts executable Postgres workflow service behavior: action ordinal assignment, durable session ordering, and service-backed persistence. Extracted core owns the command and persistence boundary contracts, not the workflow service implementation. The executable behavior remains required operations / cpk-server handoff evidence.

The frozen law asserts executable Postgres workflow service behavior: session lifecycle writes, UnitOfWork participation, and durable idempotency. Extracted core owns the command and persistence boundary contracts, not the workflow service implementation. The executable behavior remains required operations / cpk-server handoff evidence.

The frozen law asserts executable workflow service separation from graph truth stores. Extracted core owns the contract boundary naming graph-truth separation, not the mutable workflow services or stores. The executable behavior remains required operations / cpk-server handoff evidence.

summary: The operations contract batch closeout supersedes only the claim that frozen workflow-service execution belongs in extracted core. Core retains the pure command and persistence boundary contracts from #787 and #791; session execution, ordering, graph-truth separation enforcement, Postgres stores, UnitOfWork, and idempotency remain operations / cpk-server responsibilities.
